(3) hba1c at line 191, unit: % (line 196), aliases: hemoglobin_a1c, a1c, glycated_hemoglobin. (4) tc_hdl_ratio unit: ratio (line 68). (5) tg_hdl_ratio unit: ratio (line 81). (10) testosterone unit: nmol/L (line 1103). (11) testosterone_free_testosterone_ratio unit: ratio (line 1368).

CONFIRMED EXISTS and content read. Contains: g/dL (lines 12–16), mg/dL, mmol/L, nmol/L, µmol/L, mg/L, %, mmol/mol. HbA1c conversions: % → mmol/mol factor 10.929 (line 109–113); mmol/mol → % linear (slope 0.09148, intercept 2.152, lines 117–122). CONFIRMED EXISTS and content read. UnitEnum (lines 42–57) defines: MG_DL, G_DL, PERCENT, U_L, K_UL, UU_ML, MG_L, MMOL_L, UMOL_L, NMOL_L, NG_ML, MMOL_MOL, MEQ_L, RATIO. _STRICT_CONVERSION_BIOMARKERS (lines 70–78) includes: cholesterol, triglycerides, glucose, hba1c, urea, creatinine, vitamin_d — hemoglobin and hematocrit are NOT in the strict set. Passthrough branch for non-strict at lines 241–245. Reference range conversion at lines 363–399.

changes.check: CRITICAL GAP — g/L missing from units.yaml and UnitEnum
changes.result: BLOCKER RISK SURFACED. g/L is NOT defined in backend/ssot/units.yaml (only g/dL at lines 12–16; mg/L at lines 47–51 — a different unit). g/L is NOT in UnitEnum in registry.py (lines 42–57). No g/dL ↔ g/L conversion factor exists in units.yaml. Sprint Task 3 requires haemoglobin g/L coherence. Without adding g/L, _convert_with_explicit_unit will fire the passthrough branch (registry.py lines 241–245) for hemoglobin when input unit is g/L, leaving value unchanged with input unit — incoherent pair mechanism preserved. Cursor MUST add: (1) g/L unit definition to units.yaml, (2) bidirectional g/dL ↔ g/L conversion factor (×10 / ÷10) to units.yaml conversions block, (3) G_L token to UnitEnum in registry.py. These additions are within sprint scope and do not require SSOT duplication.

changes.check: CRITICAL GAP — L/L missing from units.yaml and UnitEnum
changes.result: BLOCKER RISK SURFACED. L/L is NOT defined in backend/ssot/units.yaml or UnitEnum. Sprint Task 4 requires haematocrit L/L coherence. Clinical policy decision (L/L vs %) must be made before implementation per sprint Task 4 requirements. If L/L → % conversion is needed, Cursor must add: (1) L/L unit definition to units.yaml, (2) bidirectional L/L ↔ % conversion (×100 / ÷100), (3) L_L token to UnitEnum. If policy is to keep canonical unit % and reject L/L-labelled input as incoherent, the scoring guard (unit_reference_range_incoherent) applies instead. STOP condition activates if policy cannot be resolved.

changes.result: WARNING SURFACED. biomarkers.yaml canonical key is hemoglobin (US spelling, line 644), aliases: hgb, hb. Sprint prompt uses haemoglobin throughout. Cursor MUST use canonical key hemoglobin in all backend logic. Creating a new key haemoglobin would introduce a duplicate SSOT authority source — FORBIDDEN per non-negotiables. Similarly hematocrit (US, line 659) not haematocrit. If UK lab payloads send haemoglobin as the key, an alias must be added to the existing hemoglobin entry — NOT a new SSOT entry.

changes.check: Defect mechanism — registry.py reference range mislabelling (source cross-ref)
changes.result: CONFIRMED. When value unit ≠ ref unit for a non-strict marker: registry.py lines 386–389 call _convert_with_explicit_unit on ref min/max with ref_unit. For hemoglobin (non-strict, no g/L factor): passthrough returns (130.0, 'g/L') for a 130 g/L bound. Lines 390–395 then assemble ref_converted = {min: 130, max: 175, unit: base_unit} where base_unit = 'g/dL'. Result: g/L-scale bounds (130–175) labelled as g/dL. This is the confirmed mechanism producing the incoherent pair displayed in UAT. Sprint fix must either: (a) add conversion factor so numeric bounds are actually converted, or (b) detect the passthrough case and emit unit_reference_range_incoherent before scoring.

CONFIRMED. has_ref_bounds (registry.py line 328) requires both rmin and rmax to be isinstance(int, float). If only one bound is present: block at lines 367–397 is skipped; ref_converted = dict(ref_range) at line 399 copies raw ref dict with original units. Value may have been converted. This creates value/ref unit split for one-sided ranges. Sprint Task 2 and STOP conditions correctly address this class.

changes.check: HbA1c canonical unit change — potential broad fixture drift STOP trigger
changes.result: WARNING SURFACED. hba1c SSOT canonical unit is currently % (biomarkers.yaml line 196). Sprint Task 5 states 'define canonical analytical unit for UK internal scoring, preferably mmol/mol'. Changing SSOT canonical from % to mmol/mol would affect ALL existing test fixtures and golden-gate fingerprints using hba1c. Sprint STOP condition explicitly covers this: 'STOP if correcting unit normalisation would change scores across many fixtures without migration/replay policy'. Cursor MUST audit fixture impact before changing hba1c canonical unit. If drift is broad: STOP and report per sprint instructions.

changes.result: CONFIRMED PRESENT. backend/core/scoring/rules.py _harmonise_hba1c_reference_range (lines 203–254) converts between % and mmol/mol families using the governed linear mapping from units.yaml (slope 0.09148, intercept 2.152). UNSCORED_REASON_HBA1C_UNIT_MISMATCH emitted when harmonisation fails (lines 47–48, 279–282). Sprint Task 5 builds on this path. Cursor must not duplicate or replace it — extend only.

CONFIRMED. backend/core/scoring/rules.py calculate_biomarker_score performs numeric comparison against reference bounds with no unit coherence check for non-HbA1c markers (lines 285–287, 312–328). Status is derived from frontend_status_from_lab_reference which is also unit-agnostic (primitives.py lines 96–108 per preflight §7). This is the scoring safety defect targeted by Task 2. The unit_reference_range_incoherent reason must be emitted upstream (in registry.py or orchestrator.py) before markers reach scoring.

CONFIRMED ABSENT. sentinel/packs/escaped_defects_v1.json (read in full) contains defect classes: ggt_alias_miss, bilirubin_canonical_mismatch, slug_leakage, wave1_contradiction, persisted_result_replay, narrative_compiler_why_surface, questionnaire_exercise_unknown, narrative_payload_assembly, statin_signal_isolation. No biomarker unit-range incoherence class present. Sprint Task 10 correctly adds biomarker_value_reference_unit_incoherence.

CONFIRMED. Sprint modifies: (1) registry.py — unit normalisation pipeline, (2) orchestrator.py — DTO construction and reference range sourcing, (3) scoring/rules.py and engine.py — scoring engine. All are Intelligence Core components per SOP §3. Behavioural output changes are intentional and explicitly declared (unscored markers previously silently scored will become unscored with explicit reason). Regression targets specified in Task 9. STOP conditions protect against unintended score changes on existing fixtures.

CONFIRMED PRESENT and sufficient. Key STOP conditions verified: cannot resolve canonical unit policy; haemoglobin fix requires broad SSOT migration; haematocrit policy indeterminate; HbA1c requires DTO/schema extension; fixture drift without migration/replay policy; frontend-only fix; missing conversion factor; lab-range sovereignty conflict; any KB change; any Automation Bus control-plane change; old analyses require migration.

changes.result: CONFIRMED CLEAN. Sprint explicitly states 'Do not create a second SSOT' (Task 1). No expected file creates a parallel authority for unit definitions or biomarker canonical units. Additions to units.yaml and biomarkers.yaml extend the single SSOT — they do not fork it.
NO RISK. Expected files do not include .codex/, .vscode/, AGENTS.md, or other tooling paths.

surfaced_risks.risk: g/L and L/L units are absent from units.yaml and UnitEnum. Must be added before Task 3 (haemoglobin) and Task 4 (haematocrit) can be implemented. This is within sprint scope but must not be skipped.

SSOT uses US spellings: hemoglobin (line 644) and hematocrit (line 659). Sprint prompt uses UK spellings throughout. Cursor must use the SSOT canonical keys. Do not create haemoglobin or haematocrit as new SSOT entries.
Changing hba1c canonical SSOT unit from % to mmol/mol (Task 5) is a HIGH-impact SSOT change. Cursor must audit fixture impact first. If drift is broad, STOP and report before committing.

registry.py passthrough branch (lines 241–245) currently labels ref range output with base_unit even when numeric bounds were not actually converted. Fix must ensure the output unit reflects the actual scale of the numeric bounds — not merely the SSOT base_unit label.

Task 2 unit_reference_range_incoherent reason must be emitted before scoring (in registry.py or orchestrator.py), not inside scoring engine. Adding it inside rules.py risks missing the one-sided range passthrough path which does not reach the same scoring branch.

frontend/app/(app)/results/page.tsx and BiomarkerDials.tsx must remain renderer-only. Task 8 frontend work must not introduce clinical unit conversion. Cursor must inspect current BiomarkerDials.tsx shouldSuppressReferenceRange (lines 92–105) to ensure LC-S7 defensive suppression is preserved and not widened beyond its current scope.
